questions.text: What is unique about the beginning of Surah At-Tawbah?
questions.answer: It is the only Surah in the Quran that does not begin with 'Bismillah-ir-Rahman-ir-Rahim'.

questions.text: Why is there no 'Bismillah' at the start of this Surah?
questions.answer: Mainly because it is a continuation of Surah Al-Anfal, and it begins with a declaration of 'Bar'at' (disassociation/ultimatum), which is not compatible with the theme of 'Mercy' in the Basmalah.

questions.text: Which expedition (battle) does this Surah primarily discuss?
What are the eight categories of people entitled to receive Zakat (Verse 60)?
questions.answer: The poor, the needy, those who collect Zakat, those whose hearts are to be reconciled, for freeing slaves, those in debt, in the cause of Allah, and the traveler.
The 'Mosque of Harm' built by hypocrites to cause division and serve as a base for enemies; Allah commanded the Prophet (SAW) never to stand in it.

questions.text: What is the significance of 'The Three who were left behind'?
questions.answer: Three sincere believers (including Ka'b bin Malik) who missed the Tabuk expedition without a valid excuse; they were boycotted for 50 days until Allah revealed their repentance was accepted.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the relationship between male and female believers?
questions.answer: They are 'Auliya' (allies/protectors) of one another, enjoining right and forbidding wrong.

questions.text: What is 'Al-Ghar-ul-Thani' (the second of the two) referring to in Verse 40?
questions.answer: The Prophet (SAW) and Abu Bakr (RA) in the cave of Thawr during the Hijrah.

questions.text: What is the 'sacredness' of the four months mentioned in Verse 36?
questions.answer: Wrongdoing (sin) is considered more grievous during these months, and they were traditionally months of no fighting.

questions.text: How does the Surah conclude in its description of Prophet Muhammad (SAW)?
questions.answer: As one who is deeply concerned for the believers, kind, and merciful (Ra'uf, Rahim).
